title: Rapid traversal of vast chemical space using machine learning-guided docking screens

data_scope.primary_library_compounds: 3,541,746,925 (Enamine REAL Ro4 subspace from 12.3B; Nov 2019 version)
data_scope.secondary_library_compounds: 235 million Ro4 molecules (ZINC15, for ultralarge benchmarking)
data_scope.benchmarking_set: 88 million protein-ligand complexes across 8 protein targets

core_conclusion: A workflow combining Mondrian conformal prediction with CatBoost classification on Morgan2 fingerprints reduces the computational cost of structure-based virtual screening of a 3.5-billion-compound library by more than 1,000-fold while retaining 80-90% of top-scoring compounds. Prospective application to G protein-coupled receptors identified novel D2R agonists and an A2AR-D2R dual-target ligand for Parkinson's disease, demonstrating that ML-guided traversal of multi-billion-scale chemical space unlocks complex polypharmacology inaccessible in smaller libraries.
why_it_matters: Breaks the billion-compound screening barrier with calibrated uncertainty guarantees; closes the computational-experimental loop with radioligand binding and functional assays; enables multi-target polypharmacology discovery; provides open benchmarking datasets (88M complexes, 8 targets) and code for community reuse.
why_nature: Target-agnostic, software-agnostic transferable framework; 1000-fold scale advance over existing methods with statistical validity guarantees; fully validated by experimental synthesis and binding assays; large open benchmarking infrastructure; addresses an exponentially growing bottleneck in drug discovery.
added_value_to_my_research: CP calibration guarantees -> prediction-interval guarantees for spatiotemporal ML models (MGWR, GWR)
GNN molecular representation -> physics-informed graph operators for spatial accessibility prediction
OOD detection via applicability domain -> spatial OOD alarm for geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
Active learning loop for chemical space -> iterative multi-source data fusion in 2SFCA refinement
SHAP on CatBoost CP classifier -> explainable feature attribution for spatial prediction models

related.title: Learning characteristics of graph neural networks predicting protein–ligand affinities
related.relation: GNN-based molecular representation as alternative to Morgan2 fingerprints in the CP workflow

related.title: Molecular deep learning at the edge of chemical space
related.relation: OOD detection for molecular ML models — directly extends the paper's applicability-domain concern

related.title: Traversing chemical space with active deep learning for low-data drug discovery
related.relation: Active learning loop complement to the paper's one-shot conformal prediction workflow

related.title: MISATO: machine learning dataset of protein–ligand complexes for structure-based drug discovery
related.relation: Provides protein–ligand complex dataset for GNN pre-training that could replace Morgan2 fingerprints

related.title: Machine learning-aided generative molecular design
related.relation: Generative molecular design as downstream step after CP-guided virtual screening identifies hit scaffolds

figures.caption: End-to-end ML-accelerated virtual screening pipeline from target selection and training-set docking through conformal prediction, compound ranking, synthesis, and experimental evaluation
Benchmarking of Mondrian conformal predictors across 8 targets — significance-threshold calibration, optimal efficiency, sensitivity and precision vs. training set size
CatBoost CP performance on 235M-compound library — database reduction vs. recall trade-off, UMAP projections, Tanimoto similarity, and ChEMBL ligand recall
Prospective D2R screen of 3.5 billion compounds — docking score distributions and experimentally verified D2R ligand binding modes (Ki = 3.0 and 3.8 uM)
Dual-target A2AR-D2R screen — homology model, dual-score scatterplots before/after ML prioritization, and binding modes of compound 5 (Ki,A2A ~20 uM; Ki,D2 = 14 uM)
